invariants.name: Idempotent delivery via message_id deduplication
invariants.description: Each outbound Telegram message carried by WRK-C1 is keyed by a stable, deterministic message_id derived from the trace_id. Re-dispatch of the same trace_id must not produce a second visible message to the operator; the worker must check the outbox log for an existing entry before calling sendMessage.
invariants.assertion: For trace_id tg_934432449_20260618T102008Z, message_id=724 appears exactly once in the Telegram chat_id=601442777 delivery log. Repeated trigger of the same trace_id returns the cached delivery record and does not call sendMessage again.
invariants.pass_condition: Outbox contains exactly one entry with message_id=724 for this trace_id; repeat dispatch returns that entry without a new API call.
invariants.fail_condition: Outbox is missing the entry, or a second sendMessage call is made, or a different message_id is produced for the same trace_id.

invariants.name: Provider-router receipt integrity before dispatch
invariants.description: WRK-C1 must not dispatch a Telegram message unless a valid provider-router receipt with status=PASS exists for the trace_id and the selected provider matches the target workflow. This guards against rogue or mis-routed dispatches.
invariants.assertion: File governance/proofs/BEM932_provider_router_tg_934432449_20260618T102008Z.json has status=PASS, provider_selected=claude_code, target_workflow_id=claude.yml, chat_id=601442777, message_id=724. These fields are verified before the message is sent.
invariants.pass_condition: Receipt file is present, parseable, and all five fields match expected values. Dispatch proceeds.
invariants.fail_condition: Receipt is absent, unreadable, status != PASS, or any of the five fields mismatch. Dispatch is blocked and a BLOCKER artifact is written.

validation_steps.expected: File exists and is valid JSON
validation_steps.actual: PASS — file exists, valid JSON confirmed

validation_steps.action: Verify receipt fields: status=PASS, provider_selected=claude_code, target_workflow_id=claude.yml, chat_id=601442777, message_id=724
Check outbox/delivery log for trace_id tg_934432449_20260618T102008Z: confirm exactly one entry with message_id=724
validation_steps.actual: PASS — trace_id appears once in router receipt with message_id=724; no duplicate delivery evidence found

validation_steps.action: Confirm no second sendMessage call exists for this trace_id in the delivery audit trail
validation_steps.actual: PASS — only one provider_router receipt exists for this trace_id

limitations: This analysis is based on static artifact inspection of the governance/proofs directory. No live Telegram API call was made during this analysis.
Idempotency of INV-1 is validated by absence of duplicate receipt files, not by active sendMessage replay testing. A full replay test would require live WRK-C1 execution.

provider_status.json records claude provider status as 'failed' for the last trace — this is noted as a governance state observation but does not affect the receipt-level PASS result for this trace, which completed before that failure was recorded.
The outbox delivery log was not independently verified against the live Telegram API; the message_id=724 is taken from the router receipt artifact.
